results.title: مونديال 2026: ميسي يشيد بشخصية الأرجنتين بعد فوز "خاص" على إنجلترا
results.description:
قال قائد المنتخب الأرجنتيني ليونيل ميسي إن تأهل منتخب بلاده إلى نهائي كأس العالم يعكس شخصية الفريق وروحه الجماعية، واصفا الفوز على إنجلترا بأنه يحمل سياقا تاريخيا خاصا.

وكانت إنجلترا في طريقها إلى بلوغ النهائي الثاني في تاريخها بعد عام 1966 عندما توجت بلقبها الوحيد، بعدما تقدمت بهدف أنتوني غوردون (55)، لكن الأرجنتين فعلتها كما درجت العادة في مبارياتها الثلاث الأخيرة بقلب الطاولة بهدفين لإنسو فرنانديس (85) والبديل لاوتارو مارتينيس (90+2) اثر تمريرتين حاسمتين من ميسي.

وقال ميسي عن التأهل إلى النهائي "ما يقدمه هذا المنتخب أمر لا يُصدق. إنه دليل جديد على شخصيتنا، وعزيمتنا، وروحنا الجماعية، والطريقة التي نلعب بها معا، وقوتنا، وصبرنا في إدارة المباريات. كان الأمر رائعا بحق".

وأضاف "رددنا هذا طوال البطولة، وها نحن نبلغ النهائي مجددا، للمرة الثانية على التوالي. بالطبع أنا سعيد للغاية. القصة لم تنتهِ بعد".

وعن مواجهة إنجلترا، أضاف "تحدثنا عن هذا الأمر قبل المباراة، في النهاية، كانت مجرد مباراة كرة قدم، لكنها كانت مواجهة خاصة للغاية، ولا سيما لأنها أمام إنجلترا، بكل ما تحمله من سياق تاريخي. ومن بين جميع مواجهات نصف النهائي التي كان يمكن أن نخوضها، كانت هذه المباراة التي أردنا الفوز بها بشدة".

ويأتي تلميح ميسي في أعقاب رفع لاعبي منتخب بلاده لافتة كتب عليها "جزر مالفيناس أرجنتينية" عقب فوزهم على إنجلترا، في إشارة إلى نزاع على سيادة هذه الجزر تحول إلى صراع مسلح عام 1982.

وتطالب الأرجنتين بالسيادة على جزر مالفيناس، الأرخبيل الصغير الواقع في جنوب المحيط الأطلسي، منذ احتلالها من قبل البريطانيين عام 1833.

وعن النهائي المرتقب أمام إسبانيا قال ميسي "إنه منتخب رائع يضم لاعبين مميزين، ويقدم كرة قدم بمستوى عال جدا. أعرفهم جيدا، كما أعرف فلسفتهم الكروية التي جرى بناؤها وتطويرها بعناية على مر السنين".

وأضاف "سبق أن واجهت بعض هؤلاء اللاعبين عندما كنت في برشلونة، وما زلت أتابع هذا المنتخب. ستكون مباراة خاصة للغاية، ونهائيا استثنائيا".

results.title: Syria detains former Assad officer linked to sarin attacks
results.description: Syria detains former Assad officer linked to sarin attacks The detention follows Syria’s recent reinstatement to the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons, after years of suspension. Thursday 16/07/2026 A 2013 file picture shows an activist wearing a gas mask in the Zamalka area, where activists say chemical weapons were used by forces loyal to Assad in the eastern suburbs of Damascus. DAMASCUS Syria’s new authorities have intensified their campaign to dismantle the legacy of Bashar al-Assad’s security apparatus, announcing the arrest of a former military officer accused of playing a central role in the regime’s chemical weapons programme. The interior ministry said former colonel Ahmed Habib Ali, described as an expert in chemical weapons, had been detained on charges linked to the storage of sarin gas and the manufacture of chemical munitions at Unit 417, a facility near Damascus. Officials said Ali oversaw the production of about 20 sarin-filled bombs weighing 250 kilograms each, alleging they were used in attacks carried out against Syrian towns during 2013 and 2017. The detention follows Syria’s recent reinstatement to the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons, after years of suspension over findings that Assad’s forces had used sarin and chlorine gas during the country’s civil war. International investigators have long linked the former government to a series of chemical attacks, including the August 2013 strike on rebel-held suburbs of Damascus that killed more than 1,400 people, according to US intelligence and human rights organisations. Facing the threat of US military intervention, Assad agreed later that year to dismantle Syria’s declared chemical arsenal, although subsequent investigations alleged that prohibited weapons continued to be used. The latest arrest highlights the new government’s efforts to demonstrate accountability for some of the conflict’s gravest alleged crimes while rebuilding ties with international institutions. Authorities have arrested dozens of former Assad-era officials since the former president’s overthrow in December 2024. In April, Syria’s judiciary began public trials against former members of the security establishment, with prosecutors pursuing cases that include alleged war crimes committed during the suppression of the 2011 uprising. The arrest of a senior figure allegedly connected to Syria’s chemical weapons programme is likely to draw close international attention as investigators continue to examine responsibility for one of the darkest chapters of the country’s 14-year conflict. Suggested By Editor Syria regains voting rights at chemical weapons watchdog Suggested By Editor Syria regains voting rights at chemical weapons watchdog

results.title: Saudia to launch Tokyo flights in November
results.description: JEDDAH: Saudia, the Kingdom’s national carrier, in collaboration with the Air Connectivity Program and Saudi Tourism Authority, has announced the addition of Tokyo as a new international destination within its growing global network. The new route will operate three flights a week from King Khalid International Airport in Riyadh, starting November 2026. According to Saudia, the collaboration with the Air Connectivity Program aims to strengthen links with international markets by launching new routes and increasing international flight frequencies.

results.title: Huawei Launches the latest Grid-Forming Strategy for Future Power Systems at The Smarter E 2026
results.description: MUNICH , July 16, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- In his speech at the industry convention in Munich, President of Smart PV & ESS Product Line, Huawei Digital Power, Steven Zhou, said the company's strategic focus on grid-forming, integrated solutions that reinforce grid stability to support high renewable penetration in Europe. Steven Zhou, President of Smart PV & ESS Product Line, Huawei Digital Power In Europe, the penetration of wind and solar is expected to reach 64% by 2030. At the same time, the power system is facing challenges – such as reduced grid resilience and weakened system strength – due to high dispatching complexity and the declining share of traditional synchronous generators. In response, European markets are increasing grid investment, scaling up grid-forming BESS deployment, developing new grid codes that include grid-forming requirements, and accelerating the shifting of power market from simple energy arbitrage to diversified grid ancillary services. In his speech at The Smarter E 2026, President of Smart PV & ESS Product Line, Huawei Digital Power, Steven Zhou, said these actions send a clear signal that "future PV and BESS products must evolve to become primary power source." As new energy technologies have developed into a primary power source, the industry has entered a period of active innovation, transitioning from single-point innovation to integrated innovation. Thanks to their grid-forming capability, hybrid PV-BESS solutions are accelerating the replacement of traditional thermal power plants. Key grid-forming functions – such as black start, inertia support, and short-circuit current support – have been fully validated under all operating conditions and timeframes in the power system, with the technical system becoming increasingly refined. In his speech, Zhou recounted how Huawei's strategic direction developed in response to the evolution of the grid. "Back in 2020, we introduced LUNA, the industry's first smart string energy storage system," said Zhou. "Named after the moon, LUNA was designed to watch over the night, ensuring that clean power no longer ends at sunset. And as you know, our solar inverter is named SUN. Rooted in the sun, it powers the day." Zhou said the role of energy storage has been redefined to guarantee the stability of the entire grid and announced that Huawei has officially upgraded the LUNA brand to LUTERRA. "LUTERRA draws its name from 'Terra' – the Earth. We are moving from lighting up the night, to protecting our planet." Tying into the company's new Smart PV Strategy, Huawei is leveraging its advantages in the integration of 4T (Bit, Watt, Heat, Battery) foundational technologies to continuously innovate, with the goal of accelerating the construction of a modern power system, including solutions for all user segments and scenarios. In his speech at The Smarter E 2026, Zhou also detailed highlights of C&I projects in Europe using the company's one-fits-all solution that integrates PV, BESS, EV chargers, and AI scheduling. For example, in Germany, AHS business park has achieved a 10% increase in revenue after just two years of operation of a Huawei one-fits-all solution, and in Spain, Zhou noted, "the electricity bill of a Carrefour supermarket is reduced by almost 40% and the payback period is only five years." Huawei is positioning itself for the evolution of the electricity market. Zhou explained, "In the future, the business models for BESS will become more diversified... Based on flexible hardware and software evolving capability, Huawei's one-match-all platform can adapt to all different business models and maximize benefits." With demonstrations across the world – from Spain to Mongolia, and Germany to the Philippines – proving the value proposition of Huawei's grid-forming technology, Zhou says the company's core competences "will empower our full-scenario Smart PV and BESS solutions, to bring maximum value for our customers while reinforcing grid stability in high renewable penetration."

results.title: مونديال 2026: الصحافة الأرجنتينية تحتفي بـ"الانتصار التاريخي" على إنجلترا
results.description:
احتفت الصحافة الأرجنتينية، الأربعاء، بتأهل منتخب بلادها إلى نهائي كأس العالم 2026 عقب الفوز على إنجلترا 2-1، ووصفت المواجهة بأنها "صدام تاريخي"، مؤكدة أن منتخب "ألبيسيليستي" نجح مرة أخرى في ملامسة مشاعر الأرجنتينيين.

وكتبت صحيفة "لا ناسيون"، إحدى أبرز الصحف في البلاد، أن "المنتخب لمس مجددا قلب الأرجنتين، وأقصى إنكلترا، وأصبح يحلم بلقب تاريخي جديد".

وأضافت "في مباراة مشحونة بالإثارة، لعب المنتخب وقاتل من أجل ملايين الأرجنتينيين، وحقق انتصارا سيظل خالدا في الذاكرة".

من جهتها، عنونت صحيفة كلارين: "بقلب نقي... الأرجنتين تقلب الطاولة على إنكلترا"، معتبرة أن "ألبيسيليستي رسخ مكانته كأفضل منتخب في تاريخ الرياضة الأرجنتينية".

أما صحيفة أوليه الرياضية، فوصفت اللاعبين بـ"الأبطال"، مشيدة بـ"عودة تاريخية" تخللتها "ملحمة جديدة" للمنتخب.

وفي عنوان ساخر، كتبت صحيفة باخينا/12: "من لا يقفز... لن يلعب النهائي"، في تحوير للهتاف الشهير لجماهير الأرجنتين: "من لا يقفز فهو إنجليزي".

بدوره، اختار موقع تي سي سبورتس عنوانا لافتا جاء فيه: "وحوش... الأرجنتين في النهائي، وإنكلترا ما زالت تنتظر"، في إشارة إلى التصريحات الشهيرة لمدرب إنكلترا ألف رامسي خلال كأس العالم 1966، عندما منع لاعبيه من تبادل القمصان مع لاعبي الأرجنتين بعد مباراة عنيفة في ربع النهائي، قبل أن يصفهم علنا بـ"الوحوش".

من جانبها، كتبت صحيفة بيرفيل أن "الأرجنتين وضعت إنجلترا على ركبتيها"، مؤكدة أن "البلاد تعيش أجواء احتفالية، وتترقب بشغف مواجهة الأحد المقبل أمام إسبانيا في النهائي".

results.title: Washington boosts Gulf allies with fresh potential defence sales
results.description: Washington boosts Gulf allies with fresh potential defence sales The US State Department authorised a possible $1.96 billion weapons package for Saudi Arabia and a $484 million sustainment programme for Kuwait’s C-17 transport aircraft. Thursday 16/07/2026 A US Air Force C-17A Globemaster III lands at Beijing Capital International Airport, May 11, 2026. WASHINGTON The United States has moved to reinforce its military partnerships in the Gulf by approving almost $2.5 billion in potential defence sales to Saudi Arabia and Kuwait, signalling continued support for regional allies as security challenges intensify across the Middle East. In separate notifications released on Wednesday, the US State Department authorised a possible $1.96 billion weapons package for Saudi Arabia and a $484 million sustainment programme for Kuwait’s C-17 transport aircraft, saying both sales advance US foreign policy and regional security objectives. Saudi Arabia’s proposed purchase centres on 20,000 APKWS-II precision-guidance sections that convert unguided rockets into precision-guided munitions for both air-to-air and air-to-ground missions. The broader package includes launch systems, rocket motors, warheads, fuses, spare parts, maintenance support, technical documentation and training. The principal contractor is BAE Systems. The State Department said the sale would strengthen the kingdom’s ability to defend itself while enabling closer operational integration with US and allied forces. Kuwait’s package focuses on sustaining the operational readiness of its C-17 strategic transport fleet through spare parts, maintenance equipment, software support, communications systems and technical assistance. According to Washington, Kuwait’s transport aircraft are routinely used to support US and coalition missions, making their continued availability strategically important. The approvals come during one of the most volatile periods in Gulf security in years. Fighting linked to the US-Iran conflict has spread across the region, disrupting shipping routes, threatening energy infrastructure and prompting Gulf states to strengthen their military preparedness. They also coincided with high-level military consultations in Riyadh, where Saudi Chief of General Staff Lieutenant General Fayyadh al-Ruwaili met US Central Command Deputy Commander Lieutenant General Patrick Frank to discuss expanding defence cooperation. While neither side disclosed details of the discussions, the meeting and the newly approved arms packages underline Washington’s continuing commitment to reinforcing the military capabilities of its Gulf partners as instability persists across the region.
